title: Proofpoint TAP URL Defense Click-Time Protection Follow-Through Rate
dataSources: `sourcetype=proofpoint:click` Proofpoint URL click-tracking and rewrite events (Threat Defense / TAP URL isolation)
app: Proofpoint On-Demand Add-on for Splunk (`Splunk_TA_ProofpointOD`) or Proofpoint SIEM syslog mapped to `proofpoint:click`
spl:
index=email sourcetype="proofpoint:click" earliest=-7d
| eval rewritten=coalesce(urlRewritten, URLRewritten, rewritten, "unknown")
| eval disposition=coalesce(threatStatus, ThreatStatus, disposition, "")
| eval user=coalesce(clickUser, recipient, Recipient, "")
| where rewritten="true" OR match(lower(_raw), "(?i)rewrite")
| stats count(eval(match(lower(disposition),"(?i)malicious|blocked|click.*block"))) as blocked_clicks count as total_clicks by user
| eval follow_through_pct=round(100*(total_clicks-blocked_clicks)/total_clicks,2)
| where total_clicks>=5 AND follow_through_pct>20
| sort -follow_through_pct
description: URL rewriting is only effective if users stop after the warning. Measuring how often rewritten malicious links still record a risky follow-through highlights training gaps, aggressive users, or mobile clients skipping interstitials.
value: Quantifies the human layer of TAP and drives targeted simulations or policy tightening for repeat click-through populations.
implementation: (1) Ingest Proofpoint click telemetry with rewrite and final disposition fields. (2) Align field names to your TA version; use `spath` if JSON is nested. (3) Exclude mailer daemons via lookup. (4) Baseline departments. (5) Pair with LMS or HR nudges for outliers.
visualization: Bar chart (follow-through % by user or OU), line chart (weekly rate), table of top users.

references.title: Splunkbase — Splunk Add-on for Proofpoint On Demand

grandmaExplanation: We watch how Proofpoint treats and rewrites mail, so threats and DLP events line up with what the security team already sees in their console.
knownFalsePositives: Training clicks, marketing campaigns with rewritten links, and helpdesk re-tests that look like follow-through risk.
dataModelAcceleration: Not applicable; primary SPL uses raw or vendor-normalized event fields.
